option_if_let_else.description: Lints usage of `if let Some(v) =... { y } else { x }` and `match.. { Some(v) => y, None/_ => x }` which are more idiomatically done with `Option::map_or` (if the else bit is a pure expression) or `Option::map_or_else` (if the else bit is an impure expression).

option_map_or_err_ok.title: Option Map Or Err Ok
option_map_or_err_ok.description: Nothing. This lint has been deprecated

option_map_unit_fn.title: Option Map Unit Fn
option_map_unit_fn.description: Checks for usage of `option.map(f)` where f is a function or closure that returns the unit type `()`.

option_option.title: Option Option
option_option.description: Checks for usage of `Option >` in function signatures and type definitions

or_fun_call.title: Or Fun Call
or_fun_call.description: Checks for calls to `.or(foo(..))`, `.unwrap_or(foo(..))`, `.or_insert(foo(..))` etc., and suggests to use `.or_else(|| foo(..))`, `.unwrap_or_else(|| foo(..))`, `.unwrap_or_default()` or `.or_default()` etc. instead.

or_then_unwrap.title: Or Then Unwrap
or_then_unwrap.description: Checks for `.or(…).unwrap()` calls to Options and Results.

out_of_bounds_indexing.title: Out Of Bounds Indexing
out_of_bounds_indexing.description: Checks for out of bounds array indexing with a constant index.

overly_complex_bool_expr.title: Overly Complex Bool Expr
overly_complex_bool_expr.description: Checks for boolean expressions that contain terminals that can be eliminated.

panic_in_result_fn.title: Panic In Result Fn
panic_in_result_fn.description: Checks for usage of `panic!` or assertions in a function whose return type is `Result`.

panicking_unwrap.title: Panicking Unwrap
panicking_unwrap.description: Checks for calls of `unwrap[_err]()` that will always fail.

partial_pub_fields.title: Partial Pub Fields
partial_pub_fields.description: Checks whether some but not all fields of a `struct` are public.

partialeq_to_none.title: Partialeq To None
partialeq_to_none.description: Checks for binary comparisons to a literal `Option::None`.

path_buf_push_overwrite.title: Path Buf Push Overwrite
path_buf_push_overwrite.description: Checks for push calls on `PathBuf` that can cause overwrites.

path_ends_with_ext.title: Path Ends With Ext
path_ends_with_ext.description: Looks for calls to `Path::ends_with` calls where the argument looks like a file extension.

pathbuf_init_then_push.title: Pathbuf Init Then Push
pathbuf_init_then_push.description: Checks for calls to `push` immediately after creating a new `PathBuf`.

pattern_type_mismatch.title: Pattern Type Mismatch
pattern_type_mismatch.description: Checks for patterns that aren't exact representations of the types they are applied to.

pedantic.title: Pedantic
pedantic.description: The `clippy::pedantic` group makes Clippy even more pedantic. You can enable the whole group with `#![warn(clippy::pedantic)]` in the `lib.rs`/`main.rs` of your crate. This lint group is for Clippy power users that want an in depth check of their code.

perf.title: Perf
perf.description: The `clippy::perf` group gives you suggestions on how you can increase the performance of your code. Those lints are mostly about code that the compiler can't trivially optimize, but has to be written in a slightly different way to make the optimizer job easier.

permissions_set_readonly_false.title: Permissions Set Readonly False
permissions_set_readonly_false.description: Checks for calls to `std::fs::Permissions.set_readonly` with argument `false`.

pointer_format.title: Pointer Format
pointer_format.description: Detects pointer format as well as `Debug` formatting of raw pointers or function pointers or any types that have a derived `Debug` impl that recursively contains them.

pointers_in_nomem_asm_block.title: Pointers In Nomem Asm Block
pointers_in_nomem_asm_block.description: Checks if any pointer is being passed to an asm! block with `nomem` option.

possible_missing_comma.title: Possible Missing Comma
possible_missing_comma.description: Checks for possible missing comma in an array. It lints if an array element is a binary operator expression and it lies on two lines.

possible_missing_else.title: Possible Missing Else
possible_missing_else.description: Checks for an `if` expression followed by either a block or another `if` that looks like it should have an `else` between them.

precedence.title: Precedence
precedence.description: Checks for operations where precedence may be unclear and suggests to add parentheses. It catches a mixed usage of arithmetic and bit shifting/combining operators, as well as method calls applied to closures.

precedence_bits.title: Precedence Bits
precedence_bits.description: Checks for bit shifting operations combined with bit masking/combining operators and suggest using parentheses.

print_in_format_impl.title: Print In Format Impl
print_in_format_impl.description: Checks for usage of `println`, `print`, `eprintln` or `eprint` in an implementation of a formatting trait.

print_literal.title: Print Literal
print_literal.description: This lint warns about the use of literals as `print!`/`println!` args.

print_stderr.title: Print Stderr
print_stderr.description: Checks for printing on stderr. The purpose of this lint is to catch debugging remnants.

print_stdout.title: Print Stdout
print_stdout.description: Checks for printing on stdout. The purpose of this lint is to catch debugging remnants.

print_with_newline.title: Print With Newline
print_with_newline.description: This lint warns when you use `print!()` with a format string that ends in a newline.

println_empty_string.title: Println Empty String
println_empty_string.description: This lint warns when you use `println!("")` to print a newline.

ptr_arg.title: Ptr Arg
ptr_arg.description: This lint checks for function arguments of type `&String`, `&Vec`, `&PathBuf`, and `Cow `. It will also suggest you replace `.clone()` calls with the appropriate `.to_owned()`/`to_string()` calls.

ptr_as_ptr.title: Ptr As Ptr
ptr_as_ptr.description: Checks for `as` casts between raw pointers that don't change their constness, namely `*const T` to `*const U` and `*mut T` to `*mut U`.

ptr_cast_constness.title: Ptr Cast Constness
ptr_cast_constness.description: Checks for `as` casts between raw pointers that change their constness, namely `*const T` to `*mut T` and `*mut T` to `*const T`.

ptr_offset_by_literal.title: Ptr Offset By Literal
ptr_offset_by_literal.description: Checks for usage of the `offset` pointer method with an integer literal.

ptr_offset_with_cast.title: Ptr Offset With Cast
ptr_offset_with_cast.description: Checks for usage of the `offset` pointer method with a `usize` casted to an `isize`.

pub_enum_variant_names.title: Pub Enum Variant Names
pub_enum_variant_names.description: Nothing. This lint has been deprecated

pub_underscore_fields.title: Pub Underscore Fields
pub_underscore_fields.description: Checks whether any field of the struct is prefixed with an `_` (underscore) and also marked `pub` (public)

question_mark.title: Question Mark
question_mark.description: Checks for expressions that could be replaced by the `?` operator.

question_mark_used.title: Question Mark Used
question_mark_used.description: Checks for expressions that use the `?` operator and rejects them.

range_minus_one.title: Range Minus One
range_minus_one.description: Checks for inclusive ranges where 1 is subtracted from the upper bound, e.g., `x..=(y-1)`.

range_plus_one.title: Range Plus One
range_plus_one.description: Checks for exclusive ranges where 1 is added to the upper bound, e.g., `x..(y+1)`.

range_step_by_zero.title: Range Step By Zero
range_step_by_zero.description: Nothing. This lint has been deprecated

range_zip_with_len.title: Range Zip With Len
range_zip_with_len.description: Checks for zipping a collection with the range of `0.._.len()`.

rc_buffer.title: Rc Buffer
rc_buffer.description: Checks for `Rc ` and `Arc ` when `T` is a mutable buffer type such as `String` or `Vec`.

rc_clone_in_vec_init.title: Rc Clone In Vec Init
rc_clone_in_vec_init.description: Checks for reference-counted pointers (`Arc`, `Rc`, `rc::Weak`, and `sync::Weak`) in `vec![elem; len]`

read_line_without_trim.title: Read Line Without Trim
read_line_without_trim.description: Looks for calls to [`Stdin::read_line`] to read a line from the standard input into a string, then later attempting to use that string for an operation that will never work for strings with a trailing newline character in it (e.g. parsing into a `i32`).

read_zero_byte_vec.title: Read Zero Byte Vec
read_zero_byte_vec.description: This lint catches reads into a zero-length `Vec`. Especially in the case of a call to `with_capacity`, this lint warns that read gets the number of bytes from the `Vec`'s length, not its capacity.

readonly_write_lock.title: Readonly Write Lock
readonly_write_lock.description: Looks for calls to `RwLock::write` where the lock is only used for reading.

recursive_format_impl.title: Recursive Format Impl
recursive_format_impl.description: Checks for format trait implementations (e.g. `Display`) with a recursive call to itself which uses `self` as a parameter. This is typically done indirectly with the `write!` macro or with `to_string()`.

redundant_allocation.title: Redundant Allocation
redundant_allocation.description: Checks for usage of redundant allocations anywhere in the code.

redundant_as_str.title: Redundant As Str
redundant_as_str.description: Checks for usage of `as_str()` on a `String` chained with a method available on the `String` itself.

redundant_async_block.title: Redundant Async Block
redundant_async_block.description: Checks for `async` block that only returns `await` on a future.

redundant_clone.title: Redundant Clone
redundant_clone.description: Checks for a redundant `clone()` (and its relatives) which clones an owned value that is going to be dropped without further use.

redundant_closure.title: Redundant Closure
redundant_closure.description: Checks for closures which just call another function where the function can be called directly. `unsafe` functions, calls where types get adjusted or where the callee is marked `#[track_caller]` are ignored.

redundant_closure_call.title: Redundant Closure Call
redundant_closure_call.description: Detects closures called in the same expression where they are defined.

redundant_closure_for_method_calls.title: Redundant Closure For Method Calls
redundant_closure_for_method_calls.description: Checks for closures which only invoke a method on the closure argument and can be replaced by referencing the method directly.

redundant_comparisons.title: Redundant Comparisons
redundant_comparisons.description: Checks for ineffective double comparisons against constants.

redundant_else.title: Redundant Else
redundant_else.description: Checks for `else` blocks that can be removed without changing semantics.

redundant_feature_names.title: Redundant Feature Names
redundant_feature_names.description: Checks for feature names with prefix `use-`, `with-` or suffix `-support`

redundant_field_names.title: Redundant Field Names
redundant_field_names.description: Checks for fields in struct literals where shorthands could be used.

redundant_guards.title: Redundant Guards
redundant_guards.description: Checks for unnecessary guards in match expressions.

redundant_iter_cloned.title: Redundant Iter Cloned
redundant_iter_cloned.description: Checks for calls to `Iterator::cloned` where the original value could be used instead.

redundant_locals.title: Redundant Locals
redundant_locals.description: Checks for redundant redefinitions of local bindings.

redundant_pattern.title: Redundant Pattern
redundant_pattern.description: Checks for patterns in the form `name @ _`.

redundant_pattern_matching.title: Redundant Pattern Matching
redundant_pattern_matching.description: Lint for redundant pattern matching over `Result`, `Option`, `std::task::Poll`, `std::net::IpAddr` or `bool`s

redundant_pub_crate.title: Redundant Pub Crate
redundant_pub_crate.description: Checks for items declared `pub(crate)` that are not crate visible because they are inside a private module.

redundant_slicing.title: Redundant Slicing
redundant_slicing.description: Checks for redundant slicing expressions which use the full range, and do not change the type.

redundant_static_lifetimes.title: Redundant Static Lifetimes
redundant_static_lifetimes.description: Checks for constants and statics with an explicit `'static` lifetime.

redundant_test_prefix.title: Redundant Test Prefix
redundant_test_prefix.description: Checks for test functions (functions annotated with `#[test]`) that are prefixed with `test_` which is redundant.

redundant_type_annotations.title: Redundant Type Annotations
redundant_type_annotations.description: Warns about needless / redundant type annotations.

ref_as_ptr.title: Ref As Ptr
ref_as_ptr.description: Checks for casts of references to pointer using `as` and suggests `std::ptr::from_ref` and `std::ptr::from_mut` instead.

ref_binding_to_reference.title: Ref Binding To Reference
ref_binding_to_reference.description: Checks for `ref` bindings which create a reference to a reference.

ref_option.title: Ref Option
ref_option.description: Warns when a function signature uses `&Option ` instead of `Option `.

ref_patterns.title: Ref Patterns
ref_patterns.description: Checks for usages of the `ref` keyword.

regex_creation_in_loops.title: Regex Creation In Loops
regex_creation_in_loops.description: Checks for regex compilation inside a loop with a literal.

regex_macro.title: Regex Macro
regex_macro.description: Nothing. This lint has been deprecated

renamed_function_params.title: Renamed Function Params
renamed_function_params.description: Lints when the name of function parameters from trait impl is different than its default implementation.

repeat_once.title: Repeat Once
repeat_once.description: Checks for usage of `.repeat(1)` and suggest the following method for each types.

repeat_vec_with_capacity.title: Repeat Vec With Capacity
repeat_vec_with_capacity.description: Looks for patterns such as `vec![Vec::with_capacity(x); n]` or `iter::repeat(Vec::with_capacity(x))`.

replace_box.title: Replace Box
replace_box.description: Detects assignments of `Default::default()` or `Box::new(value)` to a place of type `Box `.

replace_consts.title: Replace Consts
replace_consts.description: Nothing. This lint has been deprecated

repr_packed_without_abi.title: Repr Packed Without Abi
repr_packed_without_abi.description: Checks for items with `#[repr(packed)]`-attribute without ABI qualification

reserve_after_initialization.title: Reserve After Initialization
reserve_after_initialization.description: Informs the user about a more concise way to create a vector with a known capacity.

rest_pat_in_fully_bound_structs.title: Rest Pat In Fully Bound Structs
rest_pat_in_fully_bound_structs.description: Checks for unnecessary '..' pattern binding on struct when all fields are explicitly matched.

restriction.title: Restriction
restriction.description: The `clippy::restriction` group contains lints that will restrict you from using certain parts of the Rust language. It is not recommended to enable the whole group, but rather cherry-pick lints that are useful for your code base and your use case.

result_filter_map.title: Result Filter Map
result_filter_map.description: Checks for iterators of `Result`s using `.filter(Result::is_ok).map(Result::unwrap)` that may be replaced with a `.flatten()` call.

result_large_err.title: Result Large Err
result_large_err.description: Checks for functions that return `Result` with an unusually large `Err`-variant.

result_map_unit_fn.title: Result Map Unit Fn
result_map_unit_fn.description: Checks for usage of `result.map(f)` where f is a function or closure that returns the unit type `()`.

result_unit_err.title: Result Unit Err
result_unit_err.description: Checks for public functions that return a `Result` with an `Err` type of `()`. It suggests using a custom type that implements `std::error::Error`.

return_and_then.title: Return And Then
return_and_then.description: Detect functions that end with `Option::and_then` or `Result::and_then`, and suggest using the `?` operator instead.

return_self_not_must_use.title: Return Self Not Must Use
return_self_not_must_use.description: This lint warns when a method returning `Self` doesn't have the `#[must_use]` attribute.

reversed_empty_ranges.title: Reversed Empty Ranges
reversed_empty_ranges.description: Checks for range expressions `x..y` where both `x` and `y` are constant and `x` is greater to `y`. Also triggers if `x` is equal to `y` when they are conditions to a `for` loop.

same_functions_in_if_condition.title: Same Functions In If Condition
same_functions_in_if_condition.description: Checks for consecutive `if`s with the same function call.

same_item_push.title: Same Item Push
same_item_push.description: Checks whether a for loop is being used to push a constant value into a Vec.

same_length_and_capacity.title: Same Length And Capacity
same_length_and_capacity.description: Checks for usages of `Vec::from_raw_parts` and `String::from_raw_parts` where the same expression is used for the length and the capacity.

same_name_method.title: Same Name Method
same_name_method.description: It lints if a struct has two methods with the same name: one from a trait, another not from a trait.

search_is_some.title: Search Is Some
search_is_some.description: Checks for an iterator or string search (such as `find()`, `position()`, or `rposition()`) followed by a call to `is_some()` or `is_none()`.

seek_from_current.title: Seek From Current
seek_from_current.description: Checks if the `seek` method of the `Seek` trait is called with `SeekFrom::Current(0)`, and if it is, suggests using `stream_position` instead.

seek_to_start_instead_of_rewind.title: Seek To Start Instead Of Rewind
seek_to_start_instead_of_rewind.description: Checks for jumps to the start of a stream that implements `Seek` and uses the `seek` method providing `Start` as parameter.

self_named_constructors.title: Self Named Constructors
self_named_constructors.description: Warns when constructors have the same name as their types.

self_named_module_files.title: Self Named Module Files
self_named_module_files.description: Checks that module layout uses only `mod.rs` files.

self_only_used_in_recursion.title: Self Only Used In Recursion
self_only_used_in_recursion.description: Checks for `self` receiver that is only used in recursion with no side-effects.

semicolon_if_nothing_returned.title: Semicolon If Nothing Returned
semicolon_if_nothing_returned.description: Looks for blocks of expressions and fires if the last expression returns `()` but is not followed by a semicolon.

semicolon_inside_block.title: Semicolon Inside Block
semicolon_inside_block.description: Suggests moving the semicolon after a block to the inside of the block, after its last expression.

semicolon_outside_block.title: Semicolon Outside Block
semicolon_outside_block.description: Suggests moving the semicolon from a block's final expression outside of the block.

separated_literal_suffix.title: Separated Literal Suffix
separated_literal_suffix.description: Warns if literal suffixes are separated by an underscore. To enforce separated literal suffix style, see the `unseparated_literal_suffix` lint.

serde_api_misuse.title: Serde Api Misuse
serde_api_misuse.description: Checks for misuses of the serde API.

set_contains_or_insert.title: Set Contains Or Insert
set_contains_or_insert.description: Checks for usage of `contains` to see if a value is not present in a set like `HashSet` or `BTreeSet`, followed by an `insert`.

shadow_reuse.title: Shadow Reuse
shadow_reuse.description: Checks for bindings that shadow other bindings already in scope, while reusing the original value.

shadow_same.title: Shadow Same
shadow_same.description: Checks for bindings that shadow other bindings already in scope, while just changing reference level or mutability.

shadow_unrelated.title: Shadow Unrelated
shadow_unrelated.description: Checks for bindings that shadow other bindings already in scope, either without an initialization or with one that does not even use the original value.

short_circuit_statement.title: Short Circuit Statement
short_circuit_statement.description: Checks for the use of short circuit boolean conditions as a statement.

should_assert_eq.title: Should Assert Eq
should_assert_eq.description: Nothing. This lint has been deprecated

should_implement_trait.title: Should Implement Trait
should_implement_trait.description: Checks for methods that should live in a trait implementation of a `std` trait (see llogiq's blog post for further information) instead of an inherent implementation.

should_panic_without_expect.title: Should Panic Without Expect
should_panic_without_expect.description: Checks for `#[should_panic]` attributes without specifying the expected panic message.

significant_drop_in_scrutinee.title: Significant Drop In Scrutinee
significant_drop_in_scrutinee.description: Checks for temporaries returned from function calls in a match scrutinee that have the `clippy::has_significant_drop` attribute.

significant_drop_tightening.title: Significant Drop Tightening
significant_drop_tightening.description: Searches for elements marked with `#[clippy::has_significant_drop]` that could be early dropped but are in fact dropped at the end of their scopes. In other words, enforces the "tightening" of their possible lifetimes.

similar_names.title: Similar Names
similar_names.description: Checks for names that are very similar and thus confusing. In particular, the lint checks for names with a single character change.

single_call_fn.title: Single Call Fn
single_call_fn.description: Checks for functions that are only used once. Does not lint tests.

single_char_add_str.title: Single Char Add Str
single_char_add_str.description: Warns when using `push_str`/`insert_str` with a single-character string literal where `push`/`insert` with a `char` would work fine.

single_char_lifetime_names.title: Single Char Lifetime Names
single_char_lifetime_names.description: Checks for lifetimes with names which are one character long.

single_char_pattern.title: Single Char Pattern
single_char_pattern.description: Checks for string methods that receive a single-character `str` as an argument, e.g., `_.split("x")`.

single_component_path_imports.title: Single Component Path Imports
single_component_path_imports.description: Checking for imports with single component use path.

single_element_loop.title: Single Element Loop
single_element_loop.description: Checks whether a for loop has a single element.

single_match.title: Single Match
single_match.description: Checks for matches with a single arm where an `if let` will usually suffice.

single_match_else.title: Single Match Else
single_match_else.description: Checks for matches with two arms where an `if let else` will usually suffice.

single_option_map.title: Single Option Map
single_option_map.description: Checks for functions with method calls to `.map(_)` on an arg of type `Option` as the outermost expression.

single_range_in_vec_init.title: Single Range In Vec Init
single_range_in_vec_init.description: Checks for `Vec` or array initializations that contain only one range.

size_of_in_element_count.title: Size Of In Element Count
size_of_in_element_count.description: Detects expressions where `size_of:: ` or `size_of_val:: ` is used as a count of elements of type `T`

size_of_ref.title: Size Of Ref
size_of_ref.description: Checks for calls to `size_of_val()` where the argument is a reference to a reference.

sliced_string_as_bytes.title: Sliced String As Bytes
sliced_string_as_bytes.description: Checks for string slices immediately followed by `as_bytes`.

stable_sort_primitive.title: Stable Sort Primitive
stable_sort_primitive.description: When sorting primitive values (integers, bools, chars, as well as arrays, slices, and tuples of such items), it is typically better to use an unstable sort than a stable sort.

std_instead_of_alloc.title: Std Instead Of Alloc
std_instead_of_alloc.description: Finds items imported through `std` when available through `alloc`.

std_instead_of_core.title: Std Instead Of Core
std_instead_of_core.description: Finds items imported through `std` when available through `core`.

str_to_string.title: Str To String
str_to_string.description: This lint checks for `.to_string()` method calls on values of type `&str`.

string_add.title: String Add
string_add.description: Checks for all instances of `x + _` where `x` is of type `String`, but only if `string_add_assign` does not match.

string_add_assign.title: String Add Assign
string_add_assign.description: Checks for string appends of the form `x = x + y` (without `let`!).

string_extend_chars.title: String Extend Chars
string_extend_chars.description: Checks for the use of `.extend(s.chars())` where s is a `&str` or `String`.

string_from_utf8_as_bytes.title: String From Utf8 As Bytes
string_from_utf8_as_bytes.description: Check if the string is transformed to byte array and casted back to string.

string_lit_as_bytes.title: String Lit As Bytes
string_lit_as_bytes.description: Checks for the `as_bytes` method called on string literals that contain only ASCII characters.

string_slice.title: String Slice
string_slice.description: Checks for slice operations on strings

string_to_string.title: String To String
string_to_string.description: Nothing. This lint has been deprecated

strlen_on_c_strings.title: Strlen On C Strings
strlen_on_c_strings.description: Checks for usage of `libc::strlen` on a `CString` or `CStr` value, and suggest calling `count_bytes()` instead.

struct_excessive_bools.title: Struct Excessive Bools
struct_excessive_bools.description: Checks for excessive use of bools in structs.

struct_field_names.title: Struct Field Names
struct_field_names.description: Detects struct fields that are prefixed or suffixed by the same characters or the name of the struct itself.

style.title: Style
style.description: The `clippy::style` group is mostly about writing idiomatic code. Because style is subjective, this lint group is the most opinionated warn-by-default group in Clippy.

suboptimal_flops.title: Suboptimal Flops
suboptimal_flops.description: Looks for floating-point expressions that can be expressed using built-in methods to improve both accuracy and performance.

suspicious.title: Suspicious
suspicious.description: The `clippy::suspicious` group is similar to the correctness lints in that it contains lints that trigger on code that is really sus and should be fixed. As opposed to correctness lints, it might be possible that the linted code is intentionally written like it is.

suspicious_arithmetic_impl.title: Suspicious Arithmetic Impl
suspicious_arithmetic_impl.description: Lints for suspicious operations in impls of arithmetic operators, e.g. subtracting elements in an Add impl.

suspicious_assignment_formatting.title: Suspicious Assignment Formatting
suspicious_assignment_formatting.description: Checks for usage of the non-existent `=*`, `=!` and `=-` operators.

suspicious_command_arg_space.title: Suspicious Command Arg Space
suspicious_command_arg_space.description: Checks for `Command::arg()` invocations that look like they should be multiple arguments instead, such as `arg("-t ext2")`.

suspicious_doc_comments.title: Suspicious Doc Comments
suspicious_doc_comments.description: Detects the use of outer doc comments (`///`, `/**`) followed by a bang (`!`): `///!`

suspicious_else_formatting.title: Suspicious Else Formatting
suspicious_else_formatting.description: Checks for formatting of `else`. It lints if the `else` is followed immediately by a newline or the `else` seems to be missing.

suspicious_map.title: Suspicious Map
suspicious_map.description: Checks for calls to `map` followed by a `count`.

suspicious_op_assign_impl.title: Suspicious Op Assign Impl
suspicious_op_assign_impl.description: Lints for suspicious operations in impls of OpAssign, e.g. subtracting elements in an AddAssign impl.

suspicious_open_options.title: Suspicious Open Options
suspicious_open_options.description: Checks for the suspicious use of `OpenOptions::create()` without an explicit `OpenOptions::truncate()`.

suspicious_operation_groupings.title: Suspicious Operation Groupings
suspicious_operation_groupings.description: Checks for unlikely usages of binary operators that are almost certainly typos and/or copy/paste errors, given the other usages of binary operators nearby.

suspicious_splitn.title: Suspicious Splitn
suspicious_splitn.description: Checks for calls to [`splitn`] (https://doc.rust-lang.org/std/primitive.str.html#method.splitn) and related functions with either zero or one splits.

suspicious_to_owned.title: Suspicious To Owned
suspicious_to_owned.description: Checks for the usage of `_.to_owned()`, on a `Cow `.

suspicious_unary_op_formatting.title: Suspicious Unary Op Formatting
suspicious_unary_op_formatting.description: Checks the formatting of a unary operator on the right hand side of a binary operator. It lints if there is no space between the binary and unary operators, but there is a space between the unary and its operand.

suspicious_xor_used_as_pow.title: Suspicious Xor Used As Pow
suspicious_xor_used_as_pow.description: Warns for a Bitwise XOR (`^`) operator being probably confused as a powering. It will not trigger if any of the numbers are not in decimal.

swap_ptr_to_ref.title: Swap Ptr To Ref
swap_ptr_to_ref.description: Checks for calls to `core::mem::swap` where either parameter is derived from a pointer

swap_with_temporary.title: Swap With Temporary
swap_with_temporary.description: Checks for usage of `std::mem::swap` with temporary values.

tabs_in_doc_comments.title: Tabs In Doc Comments
tabs_in_doc_comments.description: Checks doc comments for usage of tab characters.

temporary_assignment.title: Temporary Assignment
temporary_assignment.description: Checks for construction of a structure or tuple just to assign a value in it.

test_attr_in_doctest.title: Test Attr In Doctest
test_attr_in_doctest.description: Checks for `#[test]` in doctests unless they are marked with either `ignore`, `no_run` or `compile_fail`.

tests_outside_test_module.title: Tests Outside Test Module
tests_outside_test_module.description: Triggers when a testing function (marked with the `#[test]` attribute) isn't inside a testing module (marked with `#[cfg(test)]`).

to_string_in_format_args.title: To String In Format Args
to_string_in_format_args.description: Checks for `ToString::to_string` applied to a type that implements `Display` in a macro that does formatting.

too_long_first_doc_paragraph.title: Too Long First Doc Paragraph
too_long_first_doc_paragraph.description: Checks if the first paragraph in the documentation of items listed in the module page is too long.

too_many_arguments.title: Too Many Arguments
too_many_arguments.description: Checks for functions with too many parameters.

too_many_lines.title: Too Many Lines
too_many_lines.description: Checks for functions with a large amount of lines.

toplevel_ref_arg.title: Toplevel Ref Arg
toplevel_ref_arg.description: Checks for function arguments and let bindings denoted as `ref`.

trailing_empty_array.title: Trailing Empty Array
trailing_empty_array.description: Displays a warning when a struct with a trailing zero-sized array is declared without a `repr` attribute.

trait_duplication_in_bounds.title: Trait Duplication In Bounds
trait_duplication_in_bounds.description: Checks for cases where generics or trait objects are being used and multiple syntax specifications for trait bounds are used simultaneously.

transmute_bytes_to_str.title: Transmute Bytes To Str
transmute_bytes_to_str.description: Checks for transmutes from a `&[u8]` to a `&str`.

transmute_int_to_bool.title: Transmute Int To Bool
transmute_int_to_bool.description: Checks for transmutes from an integer to a `bool`.

transmute_int_to_non_zero.title: Transmute Int To Non Zero
transmute_int_to_non_zero.description: Checks for transmutes from `T` to `NonZero `, and suggests the `new_unchecked` method instead.

transmute_null_to_fn.title: Transmute Null To Fn
transmute_null_to_fn.description: Checks for null function pointer creation through transmute.

transmute_ptr_to_ptr.title: Transmute Ptr To Ptr
transmute_ptr_to_ptr.description: Checks for transmutes from a pointer to a pointer, or from a reference to a reference.

transmute_ptr_to_ref.title: Transmute Ptr To Ref
transmute_ptr_to_ref.description: Checks for transmutes from a pointer to a reference.

transmute_undefined_repr.title: Transmute Undefined Repr
transmute_undefined_repr.description: Checks for transmutes between types which do not have a representation defined relative to each other.

transmutes_expressible_as_ptr_casts.title: Transmutes Expressible As Ptr Casts
transmutes_expressible_as_ptr_casts.description: Checks for transmutes that could be a pointer cast.

transmuting_null.title: Transmuting Null
transmuting_null.description: Checks for transmute calls which would receive a null pointer.

trivial_regex.title: Trivial Regex
trivial_regex.description: Checks for trivial regex creation (with `Regex::new`, `RegexBuilder::new`, or `RegexSet::new`).

trivially_copy_pass_by_ref.title: Trivially Copy Pass By Ref
trivially_copy_pass_by_ref.description: Checks for functions taking arguments by reference, where the argument type is `Copy` and small enough to be more efficient to always pass by value.

tuple_array_conversions.title: Tuple Array Conversions
tuple_array_conversions.description: Checks for tuple<=>array conversions that are not done with `.into()`.

type_complexity.title: Type Complexity
type_complexity.description: Checks for types used in structs, parameters and `let` declarations above a certain complexity threshold.

type_id_on_box.title: Type Id On Box
type_id_on_box.description: Looks for calls to `.type_id()` on a `Box `.

type_repetition_in_bounds.title: Type Repetition In Bounds
type_repetition_in_bounds.description: This lint warns about unnecessary type repetitions in trait bounds

unbuffered_bytes.title: Unbuffered Bytes
unbuffered_bytes.description: Checks for calls to `Read::bytes` on types which don't implement `BufRead`.

unchecked_time_subtraction.title: Unchecked Time Subtraction
unchecked_time_subtraction.description: Lints subtraction between an `Instant` and a `Duration`, or between two `Duration` values.

unconditional_recursion.title: Unconditional Recursion
unconditional_recursion.description: Checks that there isn't an infinite recursion in trait implementations.

undocumented_unsafe_blocks.title: Undocumented Unsafe Blocks
undocumented_unsafe_blocks.description: Checks for `unsafe` blocks and impls without a `// SAFETY: ` comment explaining why the unsafe operations performed inside the block are safe.

unicode_not_nfc.title: Unicode Not Nfc
unicode_not_nfc.description: Checks for string literals that contain Unicode in a form that is not equal to its NFC-recomposition.

uninhabited_references.title: Uninhabited References
uninhabited_references.description: It detects references to uninhabited types, such as `!` and warns when those are either dereferenced or returned from a function.

uninit_vec.title: Uninit Vec
uninit_vec.description: Checks for `set_len()` call that creates `Vec` with uninitialized elements. This is commonly caused by calling `set_len()` right after allocating or reserving a buffer with `new()`, `default()`, `with_capacity()`, or `reserve()`.

uninlined_format_args.title: Uninlined Format Args
uninlined_format_args.description: Detect when a variable is not inlined in a format string, and suggests to inline it.

unit_arg.title: Unit Arg
unit_arg.description: Checks for passing a unit value as an argument to a function without using a unit literal (`()`).

unit_cmp.title: Unit Cmp
unit_cmp.description: Checks for comparisons to unit. This includes all binary comparisons (like `==` and `

unit_return_expecting_ord.title: Unit Return Expecting Ord
unit_return_expecting_ord.description: Checks for functions that expect closures of type Fn(…) -> Ord where the implemented closure returns the unit type. The lint also suggests to remove the semi-colon at the end of the statement if present.

unnecessary_box_returns.title: Unnecessary Box Returns
unnecessary_box_returns.description: Checks for a return type containing a `Box ` where `T` implements `Sized`

unnecessary_cast.title: Unnecessary Cast
unnecessary_cast.description: Checks for casts to the same type, casts of int literals to integer types, casts of float literals to float types, and casts between raw pointers that don't change type or constness.

unnecessary_clippy_cfg.title: Unnecessary Clippy Cfg
unnecessary_clippy_cfg.description: Checks for `#[cfg_attr(clippy, allow(clippy::lint))]` and suggests to replace it with `#[allow(clippy::lint)]`.

unnecessary_debug_formatting.title: Unnecessary Debug Formatting
unnecessary_debug_formatting.description: Checks for `Debug` formatting (`{:?}`) applied to an `OsStr` or `Path`.

unnecessary_fallible_conversions.title: Unnecessary Fallible Conversions
unnecessary_fallible_conversions.description: Checks for calls to `TryInto::try_into` and `TryFrom::try_from` when their infallible counterparts could be used.

unnecessary_filter_map.title: Unnecessary Filter Map
unnecessary_filter_map.description: Checks for `filter_map` calls that could be replaced by `filter` or `map`. More specifically it checks if the closure provided is only performing one of the filter or map operations and suggests the appropriate option.

unnecessary_find_map.title: Unnecessary Find Map
unnecessary_find_map.description: Checks for `find_map` calls that could be replaced by `find` or `map`. More specifically it checks if the closure provided is only performing one of the find or map operations and suggests the appropriate option.

unnecessary_first_then_check.title: Unnecessary First Then Check
unnecessary_first_then_check.description: Checks the usage of `.first().is_some()` or `.first().is_none()` to check if a slice is empty.

unnecessary_fold.title: Unnecessary Fold
unnecessary_fold.description: Checks for usage of `fold` when a more succinct alternative exists. Specifically, this checks for `fold`s which could be replaced by `any`, `all`, `sum` or `product`.

unnecessary_get_then_check.title: Unnecessary Get Then Check
unnecessary_get_then_check.description: Checks the usage of `.get().is_some()` or `.get().is_none()` on std map types.

unnecessary_join.title: Unnecessary Join
unnecessary_join.description: Checks for usage of `.collect:: >().join("")` on iterators.

unnecessary_lazy_evaluations.title: Unnecessary Lazy Evaluations
unnecessary_lazy_evaluations.description: As the counterpart to `or_fun_call`, this lint looks for unnecessary lazily evaluated closures on `Option` and `Result`.

unnecessary_literal_bound.title: Unnecessary Literal Bound
unnecessary_literal_bound.description: Detects functions that are written to return `&str` that could return `&'static str` but instead return a `&'a str`.

unnecessary_literal_unwrap.title: Unnecessary Literal Unwrap
unnecessary_literal_unwrap.description: Checks for `.unwrap()` related calls on `Result`s and `Option`s that are constructed.

unnecessary_map_on_constructor.title: Unnecessary Map On Constructor
unnecessary_map_on_constructor.description: Suggests removing the use of a `map()` (or `map_err()`) method when an `Option` or `Result` is being constructed.

unnecessary_map_or.title: Unnecessary Map Or
unnecessary_map_or.description: Converts some constructs mapping an Enum value for equality comparison.

unnecessary_min_or_max.title: Unnecessary Min Or Max
unnecessary_min_or_max.description: Checks for unnecessary calls to `min()` or `max()` in the following cases

unnecessary_mut_passed.title: Unnecessary Mut Passed
unnecessary_mut_passed.description: Detects passing a mutable reference to a function that only requires an immutable reference.

unnecessary_operation.title: Unnecessary Operation
unnecessary_operation.description: Checks for expression statements that can be reduced to a sub-expression.

unnecessary_option_map_or_else.title: Unnecessary Option Map Or Else
unnecessary_option_map_or_else.description: Checks for usage of `.map_or_else()` "map closure" for `Option` type.

unnecessary_owned_empty_strings.title: Unnecessary Owned Empty Strings
unnecessary_owned_empty_strings.description: Detects cases of owned empty strings being passed as an argument to a function expecting `&str`

unnecessary_result_map_or_else.title: Unnecessary Result Map Or Else
unnecessary_result_map_or_else.description: Checks for usage of `.map_or_else()` "map closure" for `Result` type.

unnecessary_safety_comment.title: Unnecessary Safety Comment
unnecessary_safety_comment.description: Checks for `// SAFETY: ` comments on safe code.

unnecessary_safety_doc.title: Unnecessary Safety Doc
unnecessary_safety_doc.description: Checks for the doc comments of publicly visible safe functions and traits and warns if there is a `# Safety` section.

unnecessary_semicolon.title: Unnecessary Semicolon
unnecessary_semicolon.description: Checks for the presence of a semicolon at the end of a `match` or `if` statement evaluating to `()`.

unnecessary_sort_by.title: Unnecessary Sort By
unnecessary_sort_by.description: Checks for usage of `Vec::sort_by` passing in a closure which compares the two arguments, either directly or indirectly.

unnecessary_struct_initialization.title: Unnecessary Struct Initialization
unnecessary_struct_initialization.description: Checks for initialization of an identical `struct` from another instance of the type, either by copying a base without setting any field or by moving all fields individually.

unnecessary_to_owned.title: Unnecessary To Owned
unnecessary_to_owned.description: Checks for unnecessary calls to `ToOwned::to_owned` and other `to_owned`-like functions.

unnecessary_trailing_comma.title: Unnecessary Trailing Comma
unnecessary_trailing_comma.description: Suggests removing an unnecessary trailing comma before the closing parenthesis in single-line macro invocations.

unnecessary_unwrap.title: Unnecessary Unwrap
unnecessary_unwrap.description: Checks for calls of `unwrap[_err]()` that cannot fail.

unnecessary_wraps.title: Unnecessary Wraps
unnecessary_wraps.description: Checks for private functions that only return `Ok` or `Some`.

unneeded_field_pattern.title: Unneeded Field Pattern
unneeded_field_pattern.description: Checks for structure field patterns bound to wildcards.

unneeded_struct_pattern.title: Unneeded Struct Pattern
unneeded_struct_pattern.description: Checks for struct patterns that match against unit variant.

unneeded_wildcard_pattern.title: Unneeded Wildcard Pattern
unneeded_wildcard_pattern.description: Checks for tuple and struct patterns with a wildcard pattern (`_`) is next to a rest pattern (`..`).

unnested_or_patterns.title: Unnested Or Patterns
unnested_or_patterns.description: Checks for unnested or-patterns, e.g., `Some(0) | Some(2)` and suggests replacing the pattern with a nested one, `Some(0 | 2)`.

unreadable_literal.title: Unreadable Literal
unreadable_literal.description: Warns if a long integral or floating-point constant does not contain underscores.

unsafe_derive_deserialize.title: Unsafe Derive Deserialize
unsafe_derive_deserialize.description: Checks for deriving `serde::Deserialize` on a type that has methods using `unsafe`.

unsafe_removed_from_name.title: Unsafe Removed From Name
unsafe_removed_from_name.description: Checks for imports that remove "unsafe" from an item's name.

unsafe_vector_initialization.title: Unsafe Vector Initialization
unsafe_vector_initialization.description: Nothing. This lint has been deprecated

unseparated_literal_suffix.title: Unseparated Literal Suffix
unseparated_literal_suffix.description: Warns if literal suffixes are not separated by an underscore. To enforce unseparated literal suffix style, see the `separated_literal_suffix` lint.

unsound_collection_transmute.title: Unsound Collection Transmute
unsound_collection_transmute.description: Checks for transmutes between collections whose types have different ABI, size or alignment.

unstable_as_mut_slice.title: Unstable As Mut Slice
unstable_as_mut_slice.description: Nothing. This lint has been deprecated

unstable_as_slice.title: Unstable As Slice
unstable_as_slice.description: Nothing. This lint has been deprecated

unused_async.title: Unused Async
unused_async.description: Checks for functions that are declared `async` but have no `.await`s inside of them.

unused_collect.title: Unused Collect
unused_collect.description: Nothing. This lint has been deprecated

unused_enumerate_index.title: Unused Enumerate Index
unused_enumerate_index.description: Checks for uses of the `enumerate` method where the index is unused (`_`)

unused_format_specs.title: Unused Format Specs
unused_format_specs.description: Detects formatting parameters that have no effect on the output of `format!()`, `println!()` or similar macros.

unused_peekable.title: Unused Peekable
unused_peekable.description: Checks for the creation of a `peekable` iterator that is never `.peek()`ed

unused_result_ok.title: Unused Result Ok
unused_result_ok.description: Checks for calls to `Result::ok()` without using the returned `Option`.

unused_rounding.title: Unused Rounding
unused_rounding.description: Detects cases where a whole-number literal float is being rounded, using the `floor`, `ceil`, or `round` methods.

unused_self.title: Unused Self
unused_self.description: Checks methods that contain a `self` argument but don't use it

unused_trait_names.title: Unused Trait Names
unused_trait_names.description: Checks for `use Trait` where the Trait is only used for its methods and not referenced by a path directly.

unused_unit.title: Unused Unit
unused_unit.description: Checks for unit (`()`) expressions that can be removed.

unusual_byte_groupings.title: Unusual Byte Groupings
unusual_byte_groupings.description: Warns if hexadecimal or binary literals are not grouped by nibble or byte.

unwrap_in_result.title: Unwrap In Result
unwrap_in_result.description: Checks for functions of type `Result` that contain `expect()` or `unwrap()`

unwrap_or_default.title: Unwrap Or Default
unwrap_or_default.description: Checks for usages of the following functions with an argument that constructs a default value (e.g., `Default::default` or `String::new`):

unwrap_used.title: Unwrap Used
unwrap_used.description: Checks for `.unwrap()` or `.unwrap_err()` calls on `Result`s and `.unwrap()` call on `Option`s.

upper_case_acronyms.title: Upper Case Acronyms
upper_case_acronyms.description: Checks for fully capitalized names and optionally names containing a capitalized acronym.

use_debug.title: Use Debug
use_debug.description: Checks for usage of `Debug` formatting. The purpose of this lint is to catch debugging remnants.

use_self.title: Use Self
use_self.description: Checks for unnecessary repetition of structure name when a replacement with `Self` is applicable.

used_underscore_binding.title: Used Underscore Binding
used_underscore_binding.description: Checks for the use of bindings with a single leading underscore.

used_underscore_items.title: Used Underscore Items
used_underscore_items.description: Checks for the use of item with a single leading underscore.

useless_asref.title: Useless Asref
useless_asref.description: Checks for usage of `.as_ref()` or `.as_mut()` where the types before and after the call are the same.

useless_attribute.title: Useless Attribute
useless_attribute.description: Checks for `extern crate` and `use` items annotated with lint attributes.

useless_concat.title: Useless Concat
useless_concat.description: Checks that the `concat!` macro has at least two arguments.

useless_conversion.title: Useless Conversion
useless_conversion.description: Checks for `Into`, `TryInto`, `From`, `TryFrom`, or `IntoIter` calls which uselessly convert to the same type.

useless_format.title: Useless Format
useless_format.description: Checks for the use of `format!("string literal with no argument")` and `format!("{}", foo)` where `foo` is a string.

useless_let_if_seq.title: Useless Let If Seq
useless_let_if_seq.description: Checks for variable declarations immediately followed by a conditional affectation.

useless_nonzero_new_unchecked.title: Useless Nonzero New Unchecked
useless_nonzero_new_unchecked.description: Checks for `NonZero*::new_unchecked()` being used in a `const` context.

useless_transmute.title: Useless Transmute
useless_transmute.description: Checks for transmutes to the original type of the object and transmutes that could be a cast.

useless_vec.title: Useless Vec
useless_vec.description: Checks for usage of `vec![..]` when using `[..]` would be possible.

vec_box.title: Vec Box
vec_box.description: Checks for usage of `Vec >` where T: Sized anywhere in the code. Check the Box documentation for more information.

vec_init_then_push.title: Vec Init Then Push
vec_init_then_push.description: Checks for calls to `push` immediately after creating a new `Vec`.

verbose_bit_mask.title: Verbose Bit Mask
verbose_bit_mask.description: Checks for bit masks that can be replaced by a call to `trailing_zeros`

verbose_file_reads.title: Verbose File Reads
verbose_file_reads.description: Checks for usage of File::read_to_end and File::read_to_string.

volatile_composites.title: Volatile Composites
volatile_composites.description: This lint warns when volatile load/store operations (`write_volatile`/`read_volatile`) are applied to composite types.

while_float.title: While Float
while_float.description: Checks for while loops comparing floating point values.

while_immutable_condition.title: While Immutable Condition
while_immutable_condition.description: Checks whether variables used within while loop condition can be (and are) mutated in the body.

while_let_loop.title: While Let Loop
while_let_loop.description: Detects `loop + match` combinations that are easier written as a `while let` loop.

while_let_on_iterator.title: While Let On Iterator
while_let_on_iterator.description: Checks for `while let` expressions on iterators.

wildcard_dependencies.title: Wildcard Dependencies
wildcard_dependencies.description: Checks for wildcard dependencies in the `Cargo.toml`.

wildcard_enum_match_arm.title: Wildcard Enum Match Arm
wildcard_enum_match_arm.description: Checks for wildcard enum matches using `_`.

wildcard_in_or_patterns.title: Wildcard In Or Patterns
wildcard_in_or_patterns.description: Checks for wildcard pattern used with others patterns in same match arm.

write_literal.title: Write Literal
write_literal.description: This lint warns about the use of literals as `write!`/`writeln!` args.

write_with_newline.title: Write With Newline
write_with_newline.description: This lint warns when you use `write!()` with a format string that ends in a newline.

writeln_empty_string.title: Writeln Empty String
writeln_empty_string.description: This lint warns when you use `writeln!(buf, "")` to print a newline.

wrong_pub_self_convention.title: Wrong Pub Self Convention
wrong_pub_self_convention.description: Nothing. This lint has been deprecated

wrong_self_convention.title: Wrong Self Convention
wrong_self_convention.description: Checks for methods with certain name prefixes or suffixes, and which do not adhere to standard conventions regarding how `self` is taken. The actual rules are:

wrong_transmute.title: Wrong Transmute
wrong_transmute.description: Checks for transmutes that can't ever be correct on any architecture.

zero_prefixed_literal.title: Zero Prefixed Literal
zero_prefixed_literal.description: Warns if an integral constant literal starts with `0`.

zero_ptr.title: Zero Ptr
zero_ptr.description: Catch casts from `0` to some pointer type

zero_repeat_side_effects.title: Zero Repeat Side Effects
zero_repeat_side_effects.description: Checks for array or vec initializations which contain an expression with side effects, but which have a repeat count of zero.

zero_sized_map_values.title: Zero Sized Map Values
zero_sized_map_values.description: Checks for maps with zero-sized value types anywhere in the code.

zombie_processes.title: Zombie Processes
zombie_processes.description: Looks for code that spawns a process but never calls `wait()` on the child.

zst_offset.title: Zst Offset
zst_offset.description: Checks for `offset(_)`, `wrapping_`{`add`, `sub`}, etc. on raw pointers to zero-sized types

priority.description: The priority that controls which lints or [lint groups](https://doc.rust-lang.org/rustc/lints/groups.html) override other lint groups. Lower (particularly negative) numbers have lower priority, being overridden by higher numbers, and show up first on the command-line to tools like rustc.

check-cfg.description: A list of `cfg` expressions that this lint should check for.

LintLevel.title: Lint Level
LintLevel.description: Specify the [lint level](https://doc.rust-lang.org/rustc/lints/levels.html) for a lint or lint group.
